## Book Description (HTML-formatted for KDP)

<b>Before Bayouboujee. Before the Scarlet Elixir. Before any of it — there was the grind.</b>

<br>

Baron Vieux (pronounced "Vu") is not your typical sous chef. He doesn't just run the line — he runs the room. Forged in the pressure cookers of telecom sales floors, corporate kitchens, and late-night New Orleans dive bars, Baron is a man who sees every kitchen crisis as a system to be solved and every screaming executive chef as a customer to be de-escalated.

<br>

But working someone else's kitchen — executing someone else's menu, protecting someone else's empire — is slowly burning a hole in his chest.

<br>

<i>The Scarlet Foundation</i> is the origin story of a man building something on his own terms, one shift at a time. Set against the neon-soaked streets and humid pre-dawn air of the French Quarter, it is the story of the dish that started everything — <b>The Scarlet Redfish</b> — and the night a faulty oven, a Friday Mardi Gras rush, and one devastating insult from the wrong person lit the fuse on Baron's empire.

<br>

Among a cast of characters drawn from New Orleans' most legendary streets — Melpomene, Calliope, Tchoup, Elysian, Desire — Baron navigates the thin line between mastery and ownership, between executing someone else's vision and finally building his own.

<br>

<b>The money is not in the recipe. The money is in the system. Baron Vieux is about to learn the difference.</b>

<br>

<i>A prequel to the acclaimed</i> Culinary Crescendo: A Symphony of Love in the Big Easy. <i>Read the origin first, or discover where it all began after falling in love with the world.</i>

---

## Series Setup Note

**Series name:** Culinary Crescendo
**Series order:** This book is the Prequel (reads before Book 1)
**Book 1:** Culinary Crescendo: A Symphony of Love in the Big Easy

In KDP, under "Series," enter:
- Series title: Culinary Crescendo
- Volume number: 0 (or "Prequel") — KDP allows decimal numbering such as 0.5 if preferred

Setting this up as a series allows Amazon to show both books together, display "Part of the Culinary Crescendo series" on each listing, and recommend one book to buyers of the other.

## Status Update (2026-07-09)

The book now has a live Amazon listing (ASIN `B0H88D3H58`), uploaded via a separate session. This draft was very likely the source material for that submission — worth confirming the live listing's actual title/description/keywords still match what's below, since this file wasn't re-verified against the final KDP entry.

## Notes for Review (original, pre-launch)

- The description avoids plot spoilers while hitting the emotional stakes.
- "The money is not in the recipe. The money is in the system." is a strong closing line that also speaks to the FuturIQ brand audience.
- The final line connecting to the published book serves both new readers and existing fans.
- Review keyword phrases against current Amazon search data before publishing — these are based on genre and character, not paid keyword research.
- Consider A/B testing the description opening: "Before Bayouboujee" vs. opening directly with Baron in action.
